---
title: "Mortality Equations for CONUS"
author: "Greg Johnson, David Marshall, Aaron Weiskittel"
date: today
format: pdf
editor: visual
fig-format: png
---

```{r}
#| label: prepare_data
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| include: false

library( dplyr )
library( ggplot2 )
library(tidyverse)
library(sf)
library(terra)


reformat_nls <- function( nls_model_summary )
{
    mat_model <- nls_model_summary$coefficients
    
    dg_model <- as.data.frame(mat_model)
    colnames(dg_model) <- c("Coef.", "Std. error", "t-stat.", "p")
    
    return( dg_model )
}

data_path <- "F:/Projects/FVS Remodel/data/"
rds_path <- "F:/Projects/FVS Remodel/fvs_remodeling/rds/"
gis_path <- "F:/Projects/FVS Remodel/gis/"
unzip_path <- "F:/Projects/FVS Remodel/gis/Normal_1991_2020_bioclim"

species <- readRDS( file=paste0(rds_path,"species.RDS") )
trees <- readRDS( file=paste0(rds_path,"tree_mort_subset.RDS") ) 

# observation threshold
min_obs <- 5000

#fia_species <- 202
#species_name <- species$CommonName[species$SPCD == fia_species]
tree_subset <- trees |> 
               filter( !is.na(startCR) & !is.na(endHT) ) |>
               mutate( endCR=ifelse(is.na(endCR), startCR, endCR ) )
  

```


## Data

We took the FIA mortality data subset, filtered out missing data (primarily crown ratio and heights). This left `r nrow(tree_subset)` observations.

The observations are distributed among states is show in @tbl-state.

```{r}
#| label: tbl-state
#| tbl-cap: Mortality Observations by State
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false

knitr::kable( tree_subset |> group_by( State ) |> summarize( n = length(X) ) |> arrange(desc(n)) )

```

## Base Rate Mortality

Daniel Kanneman and Amos Tversky^[\tiny Kahneman D. and Tversky, A. (1973). On the Psychology of Prediction. Psychology Review. 80(4), 237-251. doi: 10.1037/h0034747] made the point that any putative model should improve upon the *base rate*. In our case, the base rate is the average mortality rate for each species accounting for no other factors. We calculate this by taking the annualized mean mortality rate for each species in the data set.

```{r}
#| label: mortality_base_rate
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| include: false

survival <- function( b, periods, startDIA, endDIA,
                      startHT, endHT, startCR, endCR, startCCH, endCCH, startCCFL, endCCFL, startBAL, endBAL )
{
  cdbh <- startDIA
  ddbh <- (endDIA - startDIA) / periods
  ccr <- startCR
  dcr <- (endCR - startCR) / periods
  ccch <- startCCH
  dcch <- (endCCH - startCCH) / periods
  cht <- startHT
  dht <- (endHT - startHT) / periods
  cccfl <- startCCFL
  dccfl <- (endCCFL - startCCFL) / periods
  cbal <- startBAL
  dbal <- (endBAL - startBAL) / periods
  max_periods <- max(periods) 
  
  # Initialize survival probability vector
  p_survive_total <- rep(1.0, length(periods))
  
  for(i in 1:max_periods) 
  {
    # Hazard / Probability of surviving this specific interval
    p_step <- ifelse( ccr > 0, 
                      1.0 - exp( -exp( b[1] ) ),
                      0 )
    
    # Only multiply if the tree is still in its observation period
    mask <- i <= periods
    p_survive_total[mask] <- p_survive_total[mask] * p_step[mask]
    
    # Update covariates
    cdbh <- cdbh + ifelse( mask, ddbh, 0.0 )
    ccr <- ccr + ifelse( mask, dcr, 0.0 )
    ccch <- ccch + ifelse( mask, dcch, 0.0 )
    cccfl <- cccfl + ifelse( mask, dccfl, 0.0 )
    cht <- cht + ifelse( mask, dht, 0.0 )
    cbal <- cbal + ifelse( mask, dbal, 0.0 )
  }
  
  return( p_survive_total )
}


# Negative Log-Likelihood
neg_log_lik <- function( b, periods, startDIA, endDIA, startHT, endHT, startCR, endCR, startCCH, endCCH, 
                                     startCCFL, endCCFL, startBAL, endBAL, status ) 
{
  # Initialize survival probability vector
  p_survive_total <- survival( b, periods, startDIA, endDIA,
                                           startHT, endHT, 
                                           startCR, endCR, 
                                           startCCH, endCCH, 
                                           startCCFL, endCCFL,
                                           startBAL, endBAL )
  
  # Calculate Likelihood: 
  # If status=1 (lived), use P. If status=0 (died), use 1-P.
  likelihood <- ifelse(status == 1, p_survive_total, 1 - p_survive_total)
  
  # Return Negative Log-Likelihood (to be minimized)
  # We add a small epsilon (1e-10) to avoid log(0)
  return(-sum(log(likelihood + 1e-10)))
}

```


```{r}
#| label: fit_species
#| eval: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| include: false

parms <- NULL
residuals <- NULL

for( i in 1:nrow(species) )
{
  if( species$n[i] > min_obs )
  {
      d <- tree_subset |> filter( SPCD == species$SPCD[i] )
    
      if( nrow(d) > 0 ) 
      {
          mort_fit <- optim(
            par = c( 1.64666578 ), 
            fn = neg_log_lik, 
            periods = d$endGROWYR - d$startGROWYR,
            startDIA = d$startDIA,
            endDIA = d$endDIA,
            startHT = d$startHT,
            endHT = d$endHT,
            startCR = d$startCR,
            endCR = d$endCR,
            startCCH = d$startCCH,
            endCCH = d$endCCH,
            startCCFL = d$startCCFL,
            endCCFL = d$endCCFL,
            startBAL = d$startBAL,
            endBAL = d$endBAL,
            status = !d$died
          ) 
          
          parms <- rbind( parms, data.frame( SPCD=species$SPCD[i], 
                                             CommonName=species$CommonName[i],
                                             n = nrow(d), 
                                             b0 = mort_fit$par[1],
                                             nll = mort_fit$value ))
          
          d$survival_hat <- survival( mort_fit$par[1], 
                                  periods = d$endGROWYR - d$startGROWYR,
                                  startDIA = d$startDIA,
                                  endDIA = d$endDIA,
                                  startHT = d$startHT,
                                  endHT = d$endHT,
                                  startCR = d$startCR,
                                  endCR = d$endCR,
                                  startCCH = d$startCCH,
                                  endCCH = d$endCCH,
                                  startCCFL = d$startCCFL,
                                  endCCFL = d$endCCFL,
                                  startBAL = d$startBAL,
                                  endBAL = d$endBAL ) 
          d$mortality_res <- ((1-d$survival_hat) - d$died)/(d$endGROWYR - d$startGROWYR)
          
          residuals <- rbind( residuals, d )
      }
  }
}

saveRDS( parms, file=paste0( rds_path, "mort_parm_base_rate.RDS") )

```

```{r}
#| label: get_base_rate_estimates
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false

parms <- readRDS( paste0( rds_path, "mort_parm_base_rate.RDS") )
residuals <- NULL
results <- list()
for( i in 1:nrow(parms) )
{
  d <- tree_subset |> filter( SPCD == parms$SPCD[i] )
  d$survival_hat <- survival( parms$b0[i], 
                                  periods = d$endGROWYR - d$startGROWYR,
                                  startDIA = d$startDIA,
                                  endDIA = d$endDIA,
                                  startHT = d$startHT,
                                  endHT = d$endHT,
                                  startCR = d$startCR,
                                  endCR = d$endCR,
                                  startCCH = d$startCCH,
                                  endCCH = d$endCCH,
                                  startCCFL = d$startCCFL,
                                  endCCFL = d$endCCFL,
                                  startBAL = d$startBAL,
                                  endBAL = d$endBAL ) 
    d$mortality_res <- ((1-d$survival_hat) - d$died)/(d$endGROWYR - d$startGROWYR)
    results[[i]] <- d 
}

residuals <- bind_rows( results )

```

The base rate estimates for `r nrow(parms)` species are distributed as shown in @fig-base_rate. Most base rate annual survival estimates are between 98 and 100%. 

```{r}
#| label: fig-base_rate
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false

parms |> ggplot( aes( 1-exp(-exp(b0)) ) ) + geom_histogram() +
  xlab( "Annual Survival Probability (Base Rate)") +
  facet_wrap( ~ifelse(SPCD < 300, "Conifer","Hardwood")) +
  ggtitle( "All Species")

```

## Base Rate Residuals

The following residual graphs show the average errors for all species over classes of possible explanatory variables. 



```{r}
#| label: residual_analysis
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false

residuals |> mutate( dbh_class = as.integer(startDIA + 0.5) ) |>
  group_by( dbh_class ) |>
  summarize( mean_res = mean( mortality_res ) ) |>
  ggplot( aes( dbh_class, mean_res ) ) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth() +
  ylim( c(-0.03, 0.03) ) +
  geom_abline( intercept = 0, slope = 0 ) +
  xlab( "DBH (inches)" ) + ylab( "Mortality Residual (pred-act)" ) + 
  ggtitle( "Mortality Residuals", subtitle = "Base Rate Model (outliers removed)" )

residuals |> mutate( ht_class = as.integer(startHT/5+0.5)*5 ) |>
  group_by( ht_class ) |>
  summarize( mean_res = mean( mortality_res ) ) |>
  ggplot( aes( ht_class, mean_res ) ) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth() +
  ylim( c(-0.03, 0.03) ) +
  geom_abline( intercept = 0, slope = 0 ) +
  xlab( "Height (feet)" ) + ylab( "Mortality Residual (pred-act)" ) + 
  ggtitle( "Mortality Residuals", subtitle = "Base Rate Model (outliers removed)" )

residuals |> mutate( ht_class = as.integer(startHT*startCR/startDIA*10+0.5)/10 ) |>
  group_by( ht_class ) |>
  summarize( mean_res = mean( mortality_res ) ) |>
  ggplot( aes( ht_class, mean_res ) ) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth() +
  ylim( c(-0.03, 0.03) ) +
  geom_abline( intercept = 0, slope = 0 ) +
  xlab( "Crown Length/DBH (feet/inches)" ) + ylab( "Mortality Residual (pred-act)" ) + 
  ggtitle( "Mortality Residuals", subtitle = "Base Rate Model (outliers removed)" )

residuals |> mutate( cr_class = as.integer(startCR*30+0.5)/30 ) |>
  group_by( cr_class ) |>
  summarize( mean_res = mean( mortality_res ) ) |>
  ggplot( aes( cr_class, mean_res ) ) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth() +
  ylim( c(-0.03, 0.03) ) +
  geom_abline( intercept = 0, slope = 0 ) +
  xlab( "Crown Ratio (fraction of total height)" ) + ylab( "Mortality Residual (pred-act)" ) + 
  ggtitle( "Mortality Residuals", subtitle = "Base Rate Model (outliers removed)")

residuals |> mutate( bal_class = as.integer(startBAL/10+0.5)*10,) |>
  group_by( bal_class ) |>
  summarize( mean_res = mean( mortality_res ) ) |>
  ggplot( aes( bal_class, mean_res ) ) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth() +
  ylim( c(-0.03, 0.03) ) +
  geom_abline( intercept = 0, slope = 0 ) +
  xlab( "Basal Area in Larger Trees (feet^2/acre)" ) + ylab( "Mortality Residual (pred-act)" ) + 
  ggtitle( "Mortality Residuals", subtitle = "Base Rate Model (outliers removed)")

residuals |> mutate( ccfl_class = as.integer(startCCFL/10+0.5)*10 ) |>
  group_by( ccfl_class ) |>
  summarize( mean_res = mean( mortality_res ) ) |>
  ggplot( aes( ccfl_class, mean_res ) ) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth() +
  ylim( c(-0.03, 0.03) ) +
  geom_abline( intercept = 0, slope = 0 ) +
  xlab( "Crown Competition Factor in Larger Trees (feet^2/acre)" ) + ylab( "Mortality Residual (pred-act)" ) + 
  ggtitle( "Mortality Residuals", subtitle = "Base Rate Model (outliers removed)")

residuals |> mutate( cch_class = as.integer(startCCH*10+0.5)/10 ) |>
  group_by( cch_class ) |>
  summarize( mean_res = mean( mortality_res ) ) |>
  ggplot( aes( cch_class, mean_res ) ) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth() +
  ylim( c(-0.03, 0.03) ) +
  geom_abline( intercept = 0, slope = 0 ) +
  xlab( "Crown Closure at Tree Tip (fraction of acre)" ) + ylab( "Mortality Residual (pred-act)" ) + 
  ggtitle( "Mortality Residuals", subtitle = "Base Rate Model (outliers removed)")

residuals |> mutate( emt_class = as.integer(EMT*10+0.5)/10 ) |>
  group_by( emt_class ) |>
  summarize( mean_res = mean( mortality_res ) ) |>
  ggplot( aes( emt_class, mean_res ) ) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth() +
  ylim( c(-0.03, 0.03) ) +
  geom_abline( intercept = 0, slope = 0 ) +
  xlab( "Extreme Minimum Temperature" ) + ylab( "Mortality Residual (pred-act)" ) + 
  ggtitle( "Mortality Residuals", subtitle = "Base Rate Model (outliers removed)")

residuals |> mutate( elev_class = as.integer(ELEV/10+0.5)*10 ) |>
  group_by( elev_class ) |>
  summarize( mean_res = mean( mortality_res ) ) |>
  ggplot( aes( elev_class, mean_res ) ) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth() +
  ylim( c(-0.03, 0.03) ) +
  geom_abline( intercept = 0, slope = 0 ) +
  xlab( "Elevation (feet)" ) + ylab( "Mortality Residual (pred-act)" ) + 
  ggtitle( "Mortality Residuals", subtitle = "Base Rate Model (outliers removed)")

residuals |> mutate( pptsm_class = as.integer(PPT_sm*10+0.5)/10 ) |>
  group_by( pptsm_class ) |>
  summarize( mean_res = mean( mortality_res ) ) |>
  ggplot( aes( pptsm_class, mean_res ) ) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth() +
  ylim( c(-0.03, 0.03) ) +
  geom_abline( intercept = 0, slope = 0 ) +
  xlab( "Summer Precipitation" ) + ylab( "Mortality Residual (pred-act)" ) + 
  ggtitle( "Mortality Residuals", subtitle = "Base Rate Model (outliers removed)")

residuals |> mutate( si_class = as.integer(SICOND*10+0.5)/10 ) |>
  group_by( si_class ) |>
  summarize( mean_res = mean( mortality_res ) ) |>
  ggplot( aes( si_class, mean_res ) ) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth() +
  ylim( c(-0.03, 0.03) ) +
  geom_abline( intercept = 0, slope = 0 ) +
  xlab( "Site Index" ) + ylab( "Mortality Residual (pred-act)" ) + 
  ggtitle( "Mortality Residuals", subtitle = "Base Rate Model (outliers removed)")


residuals |> 
  ggplot( aes( as.factor(startOWNCD), mortality_res ) ) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  geom_abline( intercept = 0, slope = 0 ) +
  ylim( c(-0.3,0.3) ) +
  xlab( "Owner Code" ) + ylab( "Mortality Residual (pred-act)" ) + 
  ggtitle( "Mortality Residuals", subtitle = "Base Rate Model (outliers removed)")


 x <- residuals |> 
  mutate( res=mortality_res ) |> 
  group_by( STATECD, UNITCD, COUNTYCD ) |> 
  summarize( mean_res=mean( res ), X=mean(X), Y=mean(Y) )

x %>% ggplot( aes( X, Y, color=Hmisc::cut2(mean_res,c(-0.01,0.01) )) ) + 
                  geom_point() + 
                  viridis::scale_color_viridis(discrete = T, option="magma") +
                  labs( color="Mean Mortality Residual", x="", y="") +
                  ggtitle( "State - Unit - County Mean Mortality Residuals" )


```


## Proposed Model

The model we propose to explain more of the mortality variation over the base rate is a Gompit function:

$$ P_{surv} = 1 - e^{(-e^{(\beta_0 + \beta_1 (cr+0.01)^{\beta_2} + \beta_3 cch^{\beta_4} )})}$$ {#eq-surv}

where:

* $P_{surv}$ : probability of survival
* $cr$ : crown ratio
* $cch$ : crown closure at tree tip

We used an integrative fitting approach to account for varying period lengths and we minimized the negative log-likelihood for each species separately.

```{r}
#| label: mortality_base_rate_cr_cch
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false

survival <- function( b, periods, startDIA, endDIA,
                       startHT, endHT, startCR, endCR, startCCH, endCCH, startCCFL, endCCFL, startBAL, endBAL ) 
{
  cdbh <- startDIA
  ddbh <- (endDIA - startDIA) / periods
  ccr <- startCR
  dcr <- (endCR - startCR) / periods
  ccch <- startCCH
  dcch <- (endCCH - startCCH) / periods
  cht <- startHT
  dht <- (endHT - startHT) / periods
  cccfl <- startCCFL
  dccfl <- (endCCFL - startCCFL) / periods
  cbal <- startBAL
  dbal <- (endBAL - startBAL) / periods
  max_periods <- max(periods) 
  
  # Initialize survival probability vector
  p_survive_total <- rep(1.0, length(periods))
  
  for(i in 1:max_periods) 
  {
    # Hazard / Probability of surviving this specific interval
    p_step <- ifelse( ccr > 0, 
                      1.0 - exp( -exp( b[1] + b[2]*(ccr+0.01)^b[3] + b[4]*ccch^b[5] ) ),
                      0 )
    
    # Only multiply if the tree is still in its observation period
    mask <- i <= periods
    p_survive_total[mask] <- p_survive_total[mask] * p_step[mask]
    
    # Update covariates
    cdbh <- cdbh + ifelse( mask, ddbh, 0.0 )
    ccr <- ccr + ifelse( mask, dcr, 0.0 )
    ccch <- ccch + ifelse( mask, dcch, 0.0 )
    cccfl <- cccfl + ifelse( mask, dccfl, 0.0 )
    cht <- cht + ifelse( mask, dht, 0.0 )
    cbal <- cbal + ifelse( mask, dbal, 0.0 )
  }
  
  return( p_survive_total )
}

```


```{r}
#| label: fit_species3
#| eval: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| include: false

parms3 <- NULL
parms3a <- NULL

# 1. Pre-split the massive dataset by the grouping variable
tree_split <- tree_subset %>% group_split(SPCD)
# Name the list elements by SPCD for lightning-fast lookups
names(tree_split) <- purrr::map_chr(tree_split, ~as.character(.x$SPCD[1]))

library(foreach)
library(doFuture)
library(future.apply)
options(future.globals.maxSize = Inf)

# 1. Set up the parallel backend
registerDoFuture()
plan(multisession) # Uses background R sessions (works on Windows, Mac, and Linux)

# 2. Run the parallel loop
i <- 1
while( i <= nrow(parms) )
{
  results <- future_lapply( c(i:min(i+4,nrow(parms))), function(j) 
  {  
    print( j )
    # Initialize empty outputs for iterations that don't meet the 'if' criteria
    parms_out <- NULL
  
    if( parms$n[j] >= min_obs ) 
    {
      p <- parms3 |> filter( SPCD == parms$SPCD[j] )
      if( nrow(p) == 0 )
      {
        p <- parms3[1,]
        p$SPCD <- parms$SPCD[j]
        p$CommonName <- parms$CommonName[j]
      }
      
      sp_id <- as.character( parms$SPCD[j] )
      d <- tree_split[[sp_id]]
      
      if( nrow(d) >= min_obs ) 
      {
        mort_fit <- optim(
          par = c(p$b0, p$b1, p$b2, p$b3, 0.3688843), 
          fn = neg_log_lik,
          method = "BFGS",
          periods = d$endGROWYR - d$startGROWYR,
          startDIA = d$startDIA, endDIA = d$endDIA,
          startHT = d$startHT,   endHT = d$endHT,
          startCR = d$startCR,   endCR = d$endCR,
          startCCH = d$startCCH, endCCH = d$endCCH,
          startCCFL = d$startCCFL, endCCFL = d$endCCFL,
          startBAL = d$startBAL, endBAL = d$endBAL,
          status = !d$died
        ) 
  
        parms_out <- data.frame(
          SPCD = p$SPCD, 
          CommonName = p$CommonName,
          n = nrow(d), 
          b0 = mort_fit$par[1],
          b1 = mort_fit$par[2],
          b2 = mort_fit$par[3],
          b3 = mort_fit$par[4],
          b4 = mort_fit$par[5],
          nll = mort_fit$value
        )
      }
    }
    
    # Return both objects as a named list for this iteration
    list(parms = parms_out ) 
  }, future.seed = TRUE)

  # 3. Clean up and combine the list of results into the final dataframes
  parms3a <- bind_rows( parms3a, lapply( results, `[[`, "parms" ) )
  
  print( i )
  i <- i + 5
}

#parms3 <- rbind( parms3, parms3a )

# 4. Close background workers
plan(sequential)
  
saveRDS( parms3, file=paste0( rds_path, "mort_parm_base_rate_cr_cch.RDS") )


```


```{r}
#| label: get_3rd_estimates
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false

parms3<- readRDS( paste0( rds_path, "mort_parm_base_rate_cr_cch.RDS") )

residuals3 <- NULL
results <- list()
for( i in 1:nrow(parms3) )
{
  d <- tree_subset |> filter( SPCD == parms3$SPCD[i] )
  d$survival_hat <- survival( c(parms3$b0[i], parms3$b1[i], parms3$b2[i], parms3$b3[i], parms3$b4[i]), 
                                  periods  = d$endGROWYR - d$startGROWYR,
                                  startDIA = d$startDIA,
                                  endDIA   = d$endDIA,
                                  startHT  = d$startHT,
                                  endHT    = d$endHT,
                                  startCR  = d$startCR,
                                  endCR    = d$endCR,
                                  startCCH = d$startCCH,
                                  endCCH   = d$endCCH,
                                  startCCFL = d$startCCFL,
                                  endCCFL  = d$endCCFL,
                                  startBAL = d$startBAL,
                                  endBAL   = d$endBAL ) 
    d$mortality_res <- ((1-d$survival_hat) - d$died)/(d$endGROWYR - d$startGROWYR)
    results[[i]] <- d 
}

residuals3 <- bind_rows( results )

```

The distributions of the parameter estimates for all species are shown below.

```{r}
#| label: 3rd_estimates
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false


parms3 |> filter( n >= min_obs ) |> ggplot( aes( b0 ) ) + geom_histogram() +
 xlab( "B0") +
  facet_wrap( ~ifelse(SPCD < 300, "Conifer","Hardwood")) +
  ggtitle( "All Species")

parms3 |> filter( n >= min_obs ) |> ggplot( aes( b1 ) ) + geom_histogram() +
  xlab( "B1") +
  facet_wrap( ~ifelse(SPCD < 300, "Conifer","Hardwood")) +
  ggtitle( "All Species")

parms3 |> filter( n >= min_obs ) |> ggplot( aes( b2 ) ) + geom_histogram() +
  xlab( "B2") +
  facet_wrap( ~ifelse(SPCD < 300, "Conifer","Hardwood")) +
  ggtitle( "All Species")

parms3 |> filter( n >= min_obs ) |> ggplot( aes( b3 ) ) + geom_histogram() +
  xlab( "B3") +
  facet_wrap( ~ifelse(SPCD < 300, "Conifer","Hardwood")) +
  ggtitle( "All Species")

parms3 |> filter( n >= min_obs ) |> ggplot( aes( b4 ) ) + geom_histogram() +
  xlab( "B4") +
  facet_wrap( ~ifelse(SPCD < 300, "Conifer","Hardwood")) +
  ggtitle( "All Species")

```

@fig-nll shows that all species' negative log likelihoods were reduced with the proposed model. 

```{r}
#| label: fig-nll
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false

parms3 |> filter( n >= min_obs ) |>
  inner_join( parms, by="SPCD", suffix=c("",".br")) |> 
  ggplot( aes( nll.br, nll ) ) + geom_point() +
  geom_abline( intercept = 0, slope=1 ) +
  ylab( "Negative Log Likelihood Revised Model") +
  xlab( "Negative Log Likelihood Base Rate") +
  ggtitle( "All Species")

```


```{r}
#| label: tbl-nlle
#| eval: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| include: false
#| tbl-cap: Species with Poorer Performance than Base Rate

suspect_species <- parms3 |> filter( n >= min_obs ) |>
  inner_join( parms, by="SPCD", suffix=c("",".br")) |> 
  mutate( difference=nll - nll.br ) |>
  filter( difference > 0 ) |> 
  arrange( difference ) |>
  select( SPCD, CommonName, n ) 

knitr::kable( suspect_species, col.names = c("SPCD", "Common Name", "N") )

```

## Proposed Model Residuals

The following residual graphs show the average errors for all species where the fit improved over the base rate over classes of possible explanatory variables.


```{r}
#| label: residual_analysis3
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false

residuals3 |> mutate( dbh_class = as.integer(startDIA + 0.5) ) |>
  group_by( dbh_class ) |>
  summarize( mean_res = mean( mortality_res ) ) |>
  ggplot( aes( dbh_class, mean_res ) ) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth() +
  ylim( c(-0.03, 0.03) ) +
  geom_abline( intercept = 0, slope = 0 ) +
  xlab( "DBH (inches)" ) + ylab( "Mortality Residual (pred-act)" ) + 
  ggtitle( "Mortality Residuals", subtitle = "Revised Model (outliers removed)" )

residuals3 |> mutate( ht_class = as.integer(startHT/5+0.5)*5 ) |>
  group_by( ht_class ) |>
  summarize( mean_res = mean( mortality_res ) ) |>
  ggplot( aes( ht_class, mean_res ) ) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth() +
  ylim( c(-0.03, 0.03) ) +
  geom_abline( intercept = 0, slope = 0 ) +
  xlab( "Height (feet)" ) + ylab( "Mortality Residual (pred-act)" ) + 
  ggtitle( "Mortality Residuals", subtitle = "Revised Model (outliers removed)" )

# residuals3 |> mutate( ht_class = as.integer(startHT*startCR/startDIA*10+0.5)/10 ) |>
#   group_by( ht_class ) |>
#   summarize( mean_res = mean( mortality_res ) ) |>
#   ggplot( aes( ht_class, mean_res ) ) +
#   geom_point() +
#   geom_smooth() +
#   ylim( c(-0.03, 0.03) ) +
#   geom_abline( intercept = 0, slope = 0 ) +
#   xlab( "Crown Length/DBH (feet/inches)" ) + ylab( "Mortality Residual (pred-act)" ) + 
#   ggtitle( "Mortality Residuals", subtitle = "Revised Model (outliers removed)" )

residuals3 |> mutate( cr_class = as.integer(startCR*30+0.5)/30 ) |>
  group_by( cr_class ) |>
  summarize( mean_res = mean( mortality_res ) ) |>
  ggplot( aes( cr_class, mean_res ) ) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth() +
  ylim( c(-0.03, 0.03) ) +
  geom_abline( intercept = 0, slope = 0 ) +
  xlab( "Crown Ratio (fraction of total height)" ) + ylab( "Mortality Residual (pred-act)" ) + 
  ggtitle( "Mortality Residuals", subtitle = "Revised Model (outliers removed)" )

residuals3 |> mutate( bal_class = as.integer(startBAL/10+0.5)*10,) |>
  group_by( bal_class ) |>
  summarize( mean_res = mean( mortality_res ) ) |>
  ggplot( aes( bal_class, mean_res ) ) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth() +
  ylim( c(-0.03, 0.03) ) +
  geom_abline( intercept = 0, slope = 0 ) +
  xlab( "Basal Area in Larger Trees (feet^2/acre)" ) + ylab( "Mortality Residual (pred-act)" ) + 
  ggtitle( "Mortality Residuals", subtitle = "Revised Model (outliers removed)" )

residuals3 |> mutate( ccfl_class = as.integer(startCCFL/10+0.5)*10 ) |>
  group_by( ccfl_class ) |>
  summarize( mean_res = mean( mortality_res ) ) |>
  ggplot( aes( ccfl_class, mean_res ) ) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth() +
  ylim( c(-0.03, 0.03) ) +
  geom_abline( intercept = 0, slope = 0 ) +
  xlab( "Crown Competition Factor in Larger Trees (feet^2/acre)" ) + ylab( "Mortality Residual (pred-act)" ) + 
  ggtitle( "Mortality Residuals", subtitle = "Revised Model (outliers removed)" )

residuals3 |> mutate( cch_class = as.integer(startCCH*10+0.5)/10 ) |>
  group_by( cch_class ) |>
  summarize( mean_res = mean( mortality_res ) ) |>
  ggplot( aes( cch_class, mean_res ) ) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth() +
  ylim( c(-0.03, 0.03) ) +
  geom_abline( intercept = 0, slope = 0 ) +
  xlab( "Crown Closure at Tree Tip (fraction of acre)" ) + ylab( "Mortality Residual (pred-act)" ) + 
  ggtitle( "Mortality Residuals", subtitle = "Revised Model (outliers removed)" )

residuals3 |> mutate( emt_class = as.integer(EMT*10+0.5)/10 ) |>
  group_by( emt_class ) |>
  summarize( mean_res = mean( mortality_res ) ) |>
  ggplot( aes( emt_class, mean_res ) ) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth() +
  ylim( c(-0.03, 0.03) ) +
  geom_abline( intercept = 0, slope = 0 ) +
  xlab( "Extreme Minimum Temperature" ) + ylab( "Mortality Residual (pred-act)" ) + 
  ggtitle( "Mortality Residuals", subtitle = "Revised Model (outliers removed)" )

residuals3 |> mutate( elev_class = as.integer(ELEV/10+0.5)*10 ) |>
  group_by( elev_class ) |>
  summarize( mean_res = mean( mortality_res ) ) |>
  ggplot( aes( elev_class, mean_res ) ) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth() +
  ylim( c(-0.03, 0.03) ) +
  geom_abline( intercept = 0, slope = 0 ) +
  xlab( "Elevation (feet)" ) + ylab( "Mortality Residual (pred-act)" ) + 
  ggtitle( "Mortality Residuals", subtitle = "Revised Model (outliers removed)" )

residuals3 |> mutate( pptsm_class = as.integer(PPT_sm*10+0.5)/10 ) |>
  group_by( pptsm_class ) |>
  summarize( mean_res = mean( mortality_res ) ) |>
  ggplot( aes( pptsm_class, mean_res ) ) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth() +
  ylim( c(-0.03, 0.03) ) +
  geom_abline( intercept = 0, slope = 0 ) +
  xlab( "Summer Precipitation" ) + ylab( "Mortality Residual (pred-act)" ) + 
  ggtitle( "Mortality Residuals", subtitle = "Revised Model (outliers removed)" )

residuals3 |> mutate( si_class = as.integer(SICOND*10+0.5)/10 ) |>
  group_by( si_class ) |>
  summarize( mean_res = mean( mortality_res ) ) |>
  ggplot( aes( si_class, mean_res ) ) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth() +
  ylim( c(-0.03, 0.03) ) +
  geom_abline( intercept = 0, slope = 0 ) +
  xlab( "Site Index" ) + ylab( "Mortality Residual (pred-act)" ) + 
  ggtitle( "Mortality Residuals", subtitle = "Revised Model (outliers removed)" )

residuals3 |> 
  ggplot( aes( as.factor(startOWNCD), mortality_res ) ) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  geom_abline( intercept = 0, slope = 0 ) +
  ylim( c(-0.3,0.3) ) +
  xlab( "Owner Code" ) + ylab( "Mortality Residual (pred-act)" ) + 
  ggtitle( "Mortality Residuals", subtitle = "Revised Model (outliers removed)" )

 x <- residuals3 |> 
  mutate( res=mortality_res ) |> 
  group_by( STATECD, UNITCD, COUNTYCD, ) |> 
  summarize( mean_res=mean( res ), X=mean(X), Y=mean(Y) )

x %>% ggplot( aes( X, Y, color=Hmisc::cut2(mean_res,c(-0.03,0.03) )) ) + 
                  geom_point() + 
                  viridis::scale_color_viridis(discrete = T, option="magma") +
                  labs( color="Mean Mortality Residual", x="", y="") +
                  ggtitle( "State - Unit - County Mean Mortality Residuals" )


```
